page: Amazon Web Services r8a.4xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor. This x86_64 architecture configuration features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs, operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor. Local storage is not included, and network connectivity is provisioned at an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in single-core operations, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 10%. Multi-core performance is also strong, with Geekbench multi-core scores in the top 10% and stress-ng multi-core in the top 25%. Memory performance is characterized by strong cached read-only small block bandwidth and a strong PassMark memory score, though uncached large block read and write bandwidths fall into the average tier. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and vector neural network instructions, which contribute to top-tier performance in image processing, text processing, and small-model LLM inference.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8a.4xlarge provides a high-density DDR5 memory configuration paired with dedicated AMD EPYC compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. The lack of bundled local storage means that storage-heavy applications must rely on network-attached storage, which may impact overall I/O latency. Given its high memory capacity and strong single- and multi-core processing capabilities, this server is suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, caching layers, and medium-scale data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r8a.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, the server is equipped with 128.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage and features an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core and multi-core performance, particularly in text processing, image processing, and small-model LLM inference. While cached memory bandwidth is strong, uncached large-block memory performance is average. This instance represents a resource-dense option for memory-bound workloads, making it well-suited for database operations, caching systems, and memory-intensive enterprise applications that rely on network-attached storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8a.4xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 128 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 8 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier single-core performance for memory-bound workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8a.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 platform featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and high-speed DDR5 memory for demanding database and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 physical cores operating at 4.5 GHz
128.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory (8.0 GB per core)
Top-tier single-core and multi-core benchmark performance
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor
No local storage included, requiring network-attached storage
Optimized for memory-intensive databases, caching, and text processing
